subject: PR3 Implementation Quality and Acceptability Review

criterion_1.name: Three-hash change enforcement (diff/rationale/tombstone)
criterion_1.details: validate_iteration_artifacts.py properly detects hash changes (demand_hash/contract_hash/decision_hash) and enforces all required iteration artifacts when hashes change. FAIL logic is clear: missing any required artifact triggers specific error codes.

criterion_2.name: FAIL conditions and exit codes clarity
criterion_2.details: Error codes are well-defined in ERROR_CODES dictionary. Clear exit codes: 0=PASS, 1=FAIL. Machine-readable ValidationResult TypedDict provides structured output for automated consumption.

criterion_3.name: Machine-consumable template fields
criterion_3.details: All templates (diff_v0, rationale_v0, tombstone_v0) have schema_version fields, structured revision/hash tracking, and consistent naming conventions. Templates are JSON-based with explicit types for automated parsing.

criterion_4.name: v0-L5 index specification compliance
criterion_4.details: Implementation matches PR3 specifications from v0-L5/index.md. Directory structure (audit/diffs/, audit/rationale/, audit/tombstones/) aligns with PR3 landing artifacts. Error codes align with SF_ITERATION_* naming convention from specification.

criterion_5.name: No unauthorized changes
criterion_5.details: No contract changes detected. Only iteration artifacts infrastructure was added (validate script + templates + directories). No越权改动 identified.

strengths: Comprehensive hash change detection across all three hash types
Specific error codes enable precise failure diagnosis
Machine-readable output format supports CI/CD integration
Template schemas follow consistent versioning pattern
Flexible pattern matching supports both revision-specific and wildcard artifact paths
Documentation references provided for all specification sources
observations: The SHA256_PATTERN regex accepts optional 'sha256:' prefix, providing flexibility for different hash formats
Rationale validation accepts both .json and .md formats, accommodating different documentation preferences
The requires_tombstone flag properly separates mandatory iteration artifacts from conditional tombstone requirements
recommendations: Consider adding JSON Schema files for the three templates to enable stricter validation
Document the expected directory naming convention for diffs (e.g., <revision_from>_to_<revision_to>)
Consider adding a --dry-run mode for validation without file operations
spec_sources_verified: docs/2026-03-04/v0-L5/三哈希口径落成validate.py 的硬校验（v0）.md
docs/2026-03-04/v0-L5/Gap Analysis v0 模板（可喂给 AI 修复 & 可被审计复核）.md
conclusion: PR3 implementation meets all acceptance criteria. The iteration artifacts validation infrastructure is complete, follows v0-L5 specifications, and provides machine-readable outputs suitable for automated gate enforcement.
